name: Microsoft Windows RCE Vulnerability - Suspicious Files
notes:
This rule detects Remote Code Execution vulnerabilities in Microsoft Exchange.

Microsoft issued "CVE-2022-41040" and "CVE-2022-41082" Exchange Server.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful File Modification

when the event matches ("File Directory" = 'C:\Users\Public' AND "Filename" IN ('all.exe', 'dump.dll','ad.exe')) OR ("File Directory" = 'C:\PerfLogs' AND "Filename" IN ('gpg-error.exe', 'cm.exe')) OR ("File Directory" = 'C:\\root' AND "Filename" = 'DrSDKCaller.exe') OR ("File Directory" = 'C:\Program Files\Common Files\system\ado' AND "Filename" = 'msado32.tlb') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Microsoft Windows RCE Vulnerability - Suspicious Files on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful File Modification

AND when the event matches ("File Directory" = 'C:\Users\Public' AND "Filename" IN ('all.exe', 'dump.dll','ad.exe')) OR ("File Directory" = 'C:\PerfLogs' AND "Filename" IN ('gpg-error.exe', 'cm.exe')) OR ("File Directory" = 'C:\\root' AND "Filename" = 'DrSDKCaller.exe') OR ("File Directory" = 'C:\Program Files\Common Files\system\ado' AND "Filename" = 'msado32.tlb') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Microsoft Windows RCE Vulnerability - Suspicious Files
Event description: Detects Remote Code Execution vulnerabilities in Microsoft Exchange.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
